Title: PIRIN2 suppresses S-type lignin accumulation in a noncellautonomous manner in Arabidopsis xylem elements

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses S-type lignin accumulation and lowers the S:G lignin ratio in xylem secondary cell walls, especially around vessel elements
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell walls of xylem vessel elements and neighboring xylem fibres in hypocotyls and stems
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides mechanical strength and water-conducting cell wall properties in xylem; lignin level/composition influences lignocellulosic recalcitrance
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive overexpression); native PRN2 promoter for complementation and PRN2:GFP localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of PRN2 increased S-type lignin content and the S:G ratio in Arabidopsis hypocotyls and stems. 2. PRN2 overexpression decreased S-type lignin content, and native-promoter complementation partially restored the mutant phenotype. 3. PRN2 promoter activity and PRN2:GFP localized to cells adjacent to vessel elements, not the vessels themselves, indicating noncell-autonomous control of vessel lignification. 4. Cell-type-resolved FT-IR/Raman analyses showed altered lignin chemistry in neighboring vessel elements and fibres. 5. prn2 mutants showed elevated expression of lignin-biosynthetic genes, especially the S-lignin-related gene F5H1, supporting transcription-associated suppression of lignification by PRN2.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed specifically in xylem cells adjacent to vessel elements in stems/hypocotyls; in roots, present in cells next to vessel elements before vessel death
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed specifically in xylem cells adjacent to vessel elements in stems/hypocotyls; in roots, present in cells next to vessel elements before vessel death
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutants; constitutive overexpression; native-promoter complementation; promoter-GUS; PRN2:GFP localization; Py-GC/MS; 2D-HSQC NMR; UHPLC-MS phenolic profiling; FT-IR microspectroscopy; Raman microspectroscopy; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil-grown plants under short-day then long-day conditions; analyses in 8-week-old hypocotyls and stems
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct growth penalty for PRN2 perturbation was reported in this paper; discussion suggests lignin engineering can affect recalcitrance and vessel integrity in general
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PRN2 is not a biosynthetic enzyme; it likely acts as a transcription-related co-regulatory factor affecting lignin-biosynthetic gene expression. Its effect is noncell-autonomous and strongest for S-lignin near vessels. Hybrid aspen PttPRN2 knockdown showed a slight tendency toward increased lignin S:G ratio.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful tool to manipulate lignin composition in a cell-type-specific/noncell-autonomous manner for reducing biomass recalcitrance while potentially preserving vessel lignification and biomass yield
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: UHPLC-MS phenolic profiling (no public accession reported)

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduction of PttPRN2 expression showed a slight tendency to increase lignin S:G ratio in woody tissues
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PttPRN2 promoter driving GUS or PttPRN2-RNAi
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary xylem/woody stem tissues, especially near vessel-associated ray cells
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PttPRN2 promoter activity in hybrid aspen was localized to ray cells neighboring vessel elements. 2. PttPRN2 RNAi lines with up to 40% reduction in expression showed a slight tendency toward higher lignin S:G ratios. 3. These data support conservation of vessel-neighbor-associated PRN2 function across species.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter activity localized specifically to ray cells neighboring vessel elements in stem secondary xylem
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter activity localized specifically to ray cells neighboring vessel elements in stem secondary xylem
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-GUS in transgenic trees; promoter-driven RNAi; lignin composition analysis in transgenic hybrid aspen
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to xylem cell wall properties and woody biomass processing characteristics
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only slight effect reported; no direct growth/yield penalty stated
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as supportive cross-species evidence rather than the main Arabidopsis core experimental system
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May enable woody biomass lignin engineering through vessel-neighbor/ray-cell-targeted manipulation
